# Grok Build GUI v0.1.2

Desktop polish release: more honest slash commands, clearer toasts, real thread tidy, browser lifecycle cleanup, and broader Chinese/English coverage.

## Highlights

- Curate the slash picker to commands the desktop actually handles (no more stub spam).
- Toast severity, dedupe, and stack limits so errors no longer look like successes.
- Make **Tidy / 整理** remove other threads after confirmation.
- Localize Git, terminal, side-task, and remaining toast strings for EN/ZH.
- Incremental terminal output append; dispose browser webviews when tabs close.
- Watch session update files with a slower poll fallback (less disk thrash).
- Earlier work in this cycle: Runtime setup/binary picker, editable Files pane, Browser URL/nav fixes.

## Downloads

- **Windows x64 Setup** — standard installer.
- **Windows x64 Portable ZIP** — extract and run without installation.
- **macOS x64 DMG** — Intel Macs, unsigned preview build.
- **macOS arm64 DMG** — Apple Silicon, unsigned preview build.
- **Linux x64 AppImage** — portable Linux application.
- **Linux x64 DEB** — Debian/Ubuntu package.
- **SHA256SUMS.txt** — checksums for all packaged artifacts.

## Runtime requirement

The desktop application still requires a local native `grok` / Grok Build runtime. Install the CLI first, or point `GROK_BINARY` at a local runtime binary.

Official install:

- Windows (PowerShell): `irm https://x.ai/cli/install.ps1 | iex`
- macOS / Linux: `curl -fsSL https://x.ai/cli/install.sh | bash`

In the desktop app, open **Settings → Account** or the Runtime setup guide to re-detect, choose a custom binary, or copy the install command.

## macOS note

The preview DMGs remain unsigned and are not notarized. On first launch:

1. In Finder, Control-click **Grok Build** and choose **Open**.
2. Confirm **Open** again in the dialog.

If macOS still blocks the app:

```bash
xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine "/Applications/Grok Build.app"
```

The app shows this Gatekeeper tip once on first macOS launch.
